coredata.dc:title: Carbon Dots/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite: Efficient synthesis and application as a novel electrochemical aptasensor for the ultrasensitive determination of aflatoxin B1
coredata.dc:creator: Huang, Qitong
Lin, Xiaofeng
Chen, Dejian
Tong, Qing-Xiao
coredata.dc:description: Developing an effective method for the detection of aflatoxin B1 (AFB1) remains an arduous task due to the high toxicity of AFB1 to a health concern. In this study, a sensitive and reliable electrochemical aptasensor based on carbon dots/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite (CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4) is constructed for the determination of AFB1. The CDs have good electrical conductivity and large specific surface areas to improve the aptasensor’s sensitivity. The α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 can not only improve the catalytic performance of the aptasensor but also have magnetism, which can realize the recovery of CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 to avoid material waste and environmental pollution. This electrochemical aptasensor can achieve a good linear (0.001–100.0 nM) and excellent detection limit (0.5 pM) for the determination of AFB1. In addition, the aptasensor was also applied to determine AFB1 in beer, rice, and peanuts, all results were in good agreement with HPLC, indicating that the electrochemical aptasensor has a broad application prospect.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Aflatoxin B1
Carbon dots, α-Fe2O3 nanoparticle
Fe3O4 nanoparticle
Electrochemical aptasensor

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: articleinfo articlenumber articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issn issnnorm itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pii piinorm pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sectiontitle sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content subj subheadings suppl tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav figure table e-component body mmlmath acknowledge affil appendices articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast grantnumber grantsponsor grantsponsorid highlightsabst primabst ref

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Detection of AFB1 in real samples

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (A) SEM image of CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4; (B) TEM image of CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4; (C) HRTEM image of CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4; (D) SAED of CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4.
CVs of GCE, CDs/GCE, CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE and S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE in 1.0 mM [Fe(CN)6]3-/4- solution containing 0.1 M KCl (scan rate of 0.1 V s−1).

(A) DPVs responses of different concentration of AFB1: a to l: 0, 0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 1.0, 5.0, 10.0, 50.0, 100.0 nM; (B) Linear relationship between I and lgCAFB1 ; (C) The reusability of the aptasensor; (D) Ipa of 0.1 nM AFB1 with the interfering substances.
(A) Synthesis of CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite, (B) CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite-based an electrochemical aptasensor for detecting AFB1 in beer, rice, and peanuts.
Comparison of the different biosensors for AFB1.

ce:table-footnote.ce:label: a
ce:table-footnote.ce:note-para: ACV: alternating current voltammetry, bPEC: photoelectrochemical, cECL: electrochemiluminescence.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Results of the determination of AFB1 in beer, rice, and peanuts (n = 8).

head.ce:title: Carbon Dots/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite: Efficient synthesis and application as a novel electrochemical aptasensor for the ultrasensitive determination of aflatoxin B1

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Key Laboratory of Prevention and Treatment of Cardiovascular and Cerebrovascular Diseases, Ministry of Education, Key Laboratory of Biomaterials and Biofabrication in Tissue Engineering of Jiangxi Province, Scientific Research Center, School of Medical and Information Engineering, Gannan Medical University, Jiangxi 341000, PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Key Laboratory of Prevention and Treatment of Cardiovascular and Cerebrovascular Diseases
Key Laboratory of Biomaterials and Biofabrication in Tissue Engineering of Jiangxi Province
School of Medical and Information Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Key Laboratory of Prevention and Treatment of Cardiovascular and Cerebrovascular Diseases, Ministry of Education, Key Laboratory of Biomaterials and Biofabrication in Tissue Engineering of Jiangxi Province, Scientific Research Center, School of Medical and Information Engineering, Gannan Medical University, Jiangxi 341000, PR China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Chemistry, Key Laboratory for Preparation and Application of Ordered Structural Material of Guangdong Province, Shantou University, Guangdong 515063, PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Key Laboratory for Preparation and Application of Ordered Structural Material of Guangdong Province

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Department of Chemistry, Key Laboratory for Preparation and Application of Ordered Structural Material of Guangdong Province, Shantou University, Guangdong 515063, PR China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: c
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Xiamen Institute of Rare-earth Materials, Haixi Institutes, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Fujian 361021, PR China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Xiamen Institute of Rare-earth Materials, Haixi Institutes, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Fujian 361021, PR China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Developing an effective method for the detection of aflatoxin B1 (AFB1) remains an arduous task due to the high toxicity of AFB1 to a health concern. In this study, a sensitive and reliable electrochemical aptasensor based on carbon dots/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite (CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4) is constructed for the determination of AFB1. The CDs have good electrical conductivity and large specific surface areas to improve the aptasensor’s sensitivity. The α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 can not only improve the catalytic performance of the aptasensor but also have magnetism, which can realize the recovery of CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 to avoid material waste and environmental pollution. This electrochemical aptasensor can achieve a good linear (0.001–100.0 nM) and excellent detection limit (0.5 pM) for the determination of AFB1. In addition, the aptasensor was also applied to determine AFB1 in beer, rice, and peanuts, all results were in good agreement with HPLC, indicating that the electrochemical aptasensor has a broad application prospect.

ce:section.ce:para: Acting as a group of food fungal mycotoxins, aflatoxins (AFs) are secondary metabolites of Aspergillus flavus and Aspergillus parasiticus (He, Sun, Pu, & Huang, 2020). The AFs mainly comprise of aflatoxin B1 (AFB1), aflatoxin B2 (AFB2), aflatoxin M1 (AFM1), aflatoxin G1 (AFG1), and aflatoxin G2 (AFG2), et al. Among them, the AFB1 is the most poisonous, which is deemed as a group I carcinogen by the International Agency for Research on Cancer (Rushing & Selim, 2019). As known, AFB1 is commonly present in some agricultural products such as rice, corn, peanuts, wheat, and so on. Accordingly, the maximum contamination level of AFB1 in foodstuffs has been stipulated by many countries. The tolerable limit of AFB1 is set as 20 ng/mL in China and the United States, 2 μg/kg in the European Union, and 5 μg/kg in Korea, respectively (Rushing & Selim, 2019; Tan et al., 2019). Thus, the valid determination of AFB1 is highly desired. Till now, many analytical methods have been applied to measure AFB1, including enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) (Zhan, Hu, Li, Huang, & Xiong, 2020), high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) (Geng, Wang, Gao, Ning, & Guan, 2018), surfaces enhanced Raman scattering (Li et al., 2020), fluorescence method (Tan et al., 2019), and so on. Nevertheless, the above methods display several disadvantages including process-complicated, time-consuming, labor-intensive, instruments-expensive, or poor sensitivity. Therefore, the design of a fast, sensitive, and economic method for the detection of AFB1 has become a research hotspot. Recently, the electrochemical sensor has attracted significant attention due to its high sensitivity, easily operation, and speediness (Beheshti-Marnani, Hatefi-Mehrjardi, & Es'haghi, 2019; Cui, Li, Wang, Guo, & Pei, 2020; Goud, Hayat, Catanante, Gobi, & Marty, 2017; Krittayavathananon & Sawangphruk, 2017; Li, Liu, Zhu, Shen, Liu, & You, 2020; Qian, Wang, & Gao, 2014; Xiong et al., 2020; Xu et al., 2018; Zhang, Lu, Yang, Guo, & Nie, 2019). It is well known that electrode-modified materials have a certain relationship with the performance of electrochemical sensors. At present, many electrode-modified materials have been applied to construct electrochemical sensors for measuring AFB1, such as Thionine-reduced graphene oxide/Au nanoparticle (THI-rGO-Au) (Li et al., 2020), electrochemical rGO/poly(5-formylindole)/Au nanoparticle (ErGO/P5FIn/Au) (Zhang et al., 2019), graphene oxide/hexamethylenediamine (GO/HMDA) (Goud et al., 2017), reduced graphene oxide (rGO) (Beheshti-Marnani et al., 2019; Krittayavathananon & Sawangphruk, 2017), GO/poly(4-vinyl pyridine) (GO/P4VP) (Cui et al., 2020), DNA tetrahedron-structured probe/HRP/polyaniline (DTP/HRP/PANI) (Xiong et al., 2020), and so on. Most of the above materials have made a lot of contributions to measure AFB1, however, the above materials were either unstable or uneconomical to synthesize.

As a member of “zero-dimensional” carbon nanomaterials, carbon dots (CDs) have excellent luminescence performance, good stability, easy preparation, non-toxicity, and so on. They have been widely applied in biosensors, cell imaging, photocatalysis, and other fields (Li, Liu, Zhang, Luo, Lin, & Jiang, 2021; Lin et al., 2021; Xu & Liu, 2021). Compared with other electrode-modified materials, CDs have the advantages of better biocompatibility, low cytotoxicity, and extraordinary electrical conductivity. Therefore, CDs have been regarded as good electrode-modified material in electrochemical biosensors. For example, we prepared Pd-Au@CDs nanocomposite for the preparation of the novel electrochemical DNA biosensor (Huang, Lin, Zhu, & Tong, 2017). Divya et al. used CDs-AgNPs to construct an electrochemical DNA biosensor with high sensitivity (Divya et al., 2019). Aftab et al. constructed an electrochemical detector of anti-HIV drug rilpivirine based on CDs co-catalyzed with MWCNTs and Ag nanoparticles (Aftab, Kurbanoglu, Ozcelikay, Bakirhan, Shah, & Ozkan, 2019).

Recently, magnetic nanoparticles have been widely used in biological fields such as magnetic resonance imaging, drug loading, and biosensors due to their advantages of easy recovery, low pollution, economy, environmental protection, and high electrocatalytic activity (Jiang, Lin, Ding, & Diao, 2018; Kuang et al., 2020; Poo-arporn, Pakapongpan, Chanlek, & Poo-arporn, 2019). They were also applied in electrochemical sensors, for example, Jiang et al. designed an enzyme-free homogeneous electrochemical DNA sensor based on Fe3O4@SiO2@β-cyclodextrin nanocomposite (Jiang et al., 2018). Poo-arporn et al. combined magnetic nanoparticles Fe3O4 with graphene to obtain magnetic nanoparticles with good electrochemical properties, which realized the determination of ractopamine in pork samples (Poo-arporn et al., 2019).

In this work, CDs were prepared by high-temperature carbonization with 5-sulfosalicylic acid and diethylene glycol as precursors. Then the as-prepared CDs were used as a surface modifier and reducing agent to synthesize the mixed crystalline magnetic of CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite through the hydrothermal method (Scheme 1 A). The CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite has both excellent properties of CDs and α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4, which could be used as a good electrode-modified material to develop a novel electrochemical aptasensor. The surface of CDs is rich in hydroxyl and carboxyl functional groups, which facilitate the interaction with the amino groups of probe DNA (S1), providing a basic platform for the construction of a new type of aptasensor. In addition, The α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 can not only improve the catalytic performance of the sensor but also have magnetism, which can realize the recovery of materials to avoid material waste and environmental pollution. Therefore, we prepared an electrochemical aptasensor based on S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 for the determination of AFB1 (Scheme 1B). The electrochemical aptasensor demonstrated ultrahigh sensitivity, excellent specificity, and an ultrawide linear range. Simultaneously, the electrochemical aptasensor was also used successfully to determine AFB1 in beer, rice, and peanuts indicating its broad application prospect.

1-ethyl-3-(3′-dimethylaminopropyl)carbodiimide (EDC), Tris-HCl, Ethylene Diamine Tetraacetic Acid (EDTA), 5-sulfosalicylic acid, diethylene glycol, and sodium acetate were obtained from Macklin Biochemical Co.; Ltd. AFB1, AFB2, AFM1, AFG1, AFG2, Methylene blue (MB), and N-hydroxysuccinimide (NHS) were obtained from sigma Sigma Aldrich. Probe DNA sequence (S1) (Goud et al., 2016): 5′-NH2-(CH2)6-TGG GGT TTT GGT GGC GGG TGG TGT ACG GGC GAG GG-3′ was purchased from Shenggong Bioengineering Co.; Ltd.

Electrochemical experiments were tested on CHI660E. Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) was tested on Gemini 300. Transmission electron microscopy (TEM), high-resolution TEM (HRTEM), and selected area electron diffraction (SAED) were carried out on JEM-100CX. X-ray diffraction (XRD) was tested on Bruker D8 Advance. The magnetic hysteresis loop was determined by MPMS (SQUID) XL-7. HPLC was measured on Waters-2695E.

1 g 5-sulfosalicylic acid was added in 20 mL diethylene glycol, after complete dissolution, the mixed-solution was heated to 200 ℃ (5 ℃/min) with an electric heating sleeve, and kept constant temperature for 5 min. After cooling, the unreacted 5-sulfosalicylic acid was removed by dialysis in a dialysis bag for two days to obtain CDs.

1 g FeCl3 and 3 g sodium acetate were added to the 20 mL as-prepared CDs. The above solution was ultrasonicated for 30 min and then heated at 200 ℃ for 12 h in the Teflon-sealed autoclave. After cooling, the product was washed ten times with ethanol. After drying, the CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite was obtained.

Firstly, GCE was polished with ɤ-Al2O3 and then rinsed with CH3CH2OH and H2O. Secondly, the GCE’s surface was covered with a 5.0 μL droplet of CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite and dried out, CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE was successfully prepared.

Firstly, CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE was put into 1 mL 50.0 μM PBS, 5.00 mM EDC, and 5.00 mM NHS mixed solution for 5 h to activate the electrode, then rinsed with TE (10.0 mM Tris-HCl/1.0 mM EDTA). Secondly, the above electrode was put into 1 mL 0.1 μM S1 for 12 h, then, TE buffer and water were used to wash the electrode.

The S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE was immersed in 50.0 μM MB and 0.1 M PBS solution (pH 7.0). When the MB was enriched and saturated on the working electrode’s surface, the working electrode was rinsed by the PBS solution, then the MB/S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE was prepared successfully for the determination of AFB1. AFB1 could interact with S1, and MB would be removed from S1, which weakened the electrochemical signal of the system.
Detection of AFB1 by HPLC refers to Herzallah’s method (Herzallah, 2009), the detailed steps are in the Supplementary Materials.

As shown in Fig. S1
A, TEM characterized the CDs’ morphology, the sizes of the synthesized CDs are relatively uniform with a diameter of 1–5 nm. Fig. S1
B shows the CDs’ fluorescence spectra, the absorption spectrum of CDs has a strong and narrow peak at 350 nm and an emission peak at 460 nm. These fluorescent characteristics of CDs are consistent with previous reports (Luo et al., 2018).

Figure 1 A and 1B show the SEM and TEM images of CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite, respectively. The CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite is spherical with uniform particle distribution and the nanocomposite does not agglomerate obviously. Due to some abundant groups on the CDs’ surface, including hydroxyl carboxyl, and sulfonic groups, which are similar to the small organic molecules, they can be used as surface modifiers to synthesize monodisperse and uniform nanomaterials. Fig. 1C shows the HRTEM image of CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4, indicating that CDs are on the surface of α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4. SAED further shows that the CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 is a montmorillonite nanocomposite (Fig. 1D).

The Fourier transform infrared (FTIR) spectrum of CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite was also measured as shown in Fig. S2 , the peaks at 3437 cm−1, 1708 cm−1, and 1400 cm−1 and 560 cm−1 are aroused from the stretching vibrations of –OH, CO, CC, and Fe-O, respectively (Thu et al., 2018) The bands at 2977 cm−1 and 2910 cm−1 are saturated stretching vibrations of CH. The main characteristic peaks of the sulfonic group are at 1190 cm−1, 1038 cm−1, and 530 cm−1 (Liu, Liu, Li, Yu, & He, 2019), a vibration for CS has been also displayed at 620 cm−1 (Wen, Ma, Chen, Shen, Li, & Liu, 2015). What’s more, the peak of 1040 cm−1 is the characteristic peak of the sulfonic group in CDs (Silva, Peixoto, & Freire, 2018), which indicates that the sulfonic group exists in composite materials. Because of the abundant hydrophilic groups such as carboxyl, hydroxyl, and sulfonic groups, the CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite has excellent water solubility.

The X-ray diffraction (XRD) pattern of CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite was also studied in this work. As shown in Fig. S3 , the peaks at (012), (104), (110), (113), (024), (116), (018), (214), and (300) were the crystal surface diffraction peaks of α-Fe2O3, the results are broadly in line with the standard XRD data of α-Fe2O3 (JCPDS card, No. 33–0064) (Zhang, Wu, Xiao, Zhou, Ren, & Jiang, 2011). At the same time, the crystal surface diffraction peaks of Fe3O4 were at (220), (311), (222), (400), (422), (511), and (440), which cohered with Fe3O4′s standard XRD data (JCPDS card, No. 99–0073) (Xiao et al., 2021). In Fig. S3 , it could be seen that the peaks of (311)/(110), (511)/(018), and (440)/(214) are relatively wide, indicating that the α-Fe2O3 was the main crystal phase in the CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite.

Figure S4 shows the magnetic hysteresis loop of CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite at 300 K. It can be seen that the magnetic hysteresis loop of the nanocomposite does not have coercivity and remanence, which indicates that the CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite as superparamagnetism. Furthermore, the saturation magnetization of the CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite is 42 emu/g, which is much lower than that of the saturation magnetization of Fe3O4 (73–78 emu/g). This may be due to the quantum size effect of the nanoparticles. Within the nanoscale, the saturation magnetization decreases as the particle diameter decreases.

Meanwhile, the working electrodes were further studied by electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS). Fig.S5 shows the impedance of the different working electrodes of bare GCE, CDs/GCE, CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE, and S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE in 1.0 mM [Fe(CN)6]3-/4-. When the GCE’s surface was modified with CDs, its impedance decreases significantly. When the surface of GCE is modified with CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE, its impedance further decreases. However, when the electrode is modified with S1, the impedance increases, which is consistent with the results of CVs. What’s more, when the S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE reacts with 100.0 nM AFB1, the impedance increases further ( Fig. S6 ). Therefore, the CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE can increase the electrode’s surface area and enhance the electronic conduction performance. At the same time, the S1 is well anchored on the CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE, and the S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE can be used as an electrical analysis sensing platform for AFB1 detection.

As known, MB is often widely used as an indicator of electrochemical aptasensor because MB could react with guanine in DNA (Huang et al., 2017; Rossetti et al., 2020). In this study, MB was also applied to analyze the electrochemical aptasensor. Fig. S7 shows the relationship between Ipa and the accumulation time of MB on the S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE. the Ipa increases with the increase of MB’s accumulation time and the Ipa tends to be stable after 50 min, indicating that the S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE has a saturation accumulation time of 50 min for MB. In this study, 55 min was chosen as the accumulation time of MB on the S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE.

To study the control factors of MB electron transfer on the S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE. The effect of scan rates (v) was investigated by CVs in the 10 μM MB. Fig. S8
A shows the peak currents (I) increased with the increase of scan rates (0.01 to 0.3 V s−1). It has good linearity between I and v ( Fig. S8
B): Ipa = -150.516 v + 0.230 (R2 = 0.996) and Ipc = 105.549 v + 2.225 (R2 = 0.998, n = 6). The result indicates that the reaction of MB on the S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE is controlled by the surface-controlled process (Huang et al. 2017).

It is well known that DPVs are more sensitive than CVs. Therefore, the performance of the aptasensor was further studied by DPVs. As shown in Fig. 3 A, the S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE has a strong signal after interacting with the MB solution. However, when it hybridizes with AFB1, the electrochemical signal also decreases. The main reason is that S1 could react with AFB1, which could hinder the action of MB and guanine, causing the MB to fall off from S1 and leading to the decrease of electrochemical signals. Fig. 3B shows that when the concentration of AFB1 ranged from 0.001 to 100.0 nM, the lgCAFB1 has a good linear relationship with Ipa : Ipa = 0.722 lgCAFB1 + 2.132 (n = 8, R2 = 0.998). What’s more, the detection limit of this method reaches as low as 0.5 pM, which has higher sensitivity than those previously reported (Table 1 ). As known, the relative standard deviation (RSD) can account for the precision of the electrochemical aptasensor, the RSD can be calculated as follows:
RSD % =
∑
i = 1
n
x i
-
x -
2
n - 1
x -
× 100 %

Moreover, the average relative deviation (ARD) can reflect the accuracy of the aptasensor, it can be calculated as follows:
ARD % =
∑
i = 1
n
x i
-
x -
n ×
x -
× 100 %

As shown in Fig. 3C, ten different S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE electrodes were used to measure 0.1 nM AFB1. After calculation, the RSD and the ARD of the aptasensor are1.76% and 1.47%, respectively, which shows that this electrochemical aptasensor has good precision, accuracy, and repeatability.

The stability of the electrochemical aptasensor has been also studied. The S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE was placed in a dryer at 4 °C, and the above working electrode was applied to measure AFB1 every day. As shown in Fig. S9 , after one week, the Ipa was only dropped by 0.40%, which shows the electrochemical aptasensor has good stability.

To study the selectivity of this electrochemical aptasensor, some interferences including AFB2, AFM1, AFG1, AFG2, miscellaneous aspergillin (ST), ochratoxin A (OA), and fumonisin B1 (FB1) were employed. As shown in Fig. 3D, when the concentration of interferences is 50 times that of AFB1, the interferences can be almost ignored.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Detection of AFB1 in real samples
ce:section.ce:para: To investigate the practical application of this aptasensor, this aptasensor was applied to determine AFB1 in beer, rice, and peanut. The samples processing was referred to previous reports (Beloglazova & Eremin, 2015; Sabet, Hosseini, Khabbaz, Dadmehr, & Ganjali, 2017). As known, the recovery studies, RSD and ARD can verify the accuracy and sensitivity of the aptasensor in actual application. As shown in Table 2 , the recovery rate of the above samples is the range between 99.45% and 106.66%, the RSD is in the range of 1.43 to 2.30, and the ARD is in the range of 1.16–2.03%, indicating that this aptasensor has good accuracy in practical applications. What’s more, all results are in good agreement with HPLC. All of the above results indicate that this aptasensor has a high degree of accuracy in real samples.

In this work, the CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite was prepared by a simple, green hydrothermal method. CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite was modified on the GCE to construct a new electrochemical aptasensor for the determination of AFB1 with ultrahigh sensitivity (0.5 pM), good stability, excellent specificity, and ultrawide linear range. What’s more, the electrochemical aptasensor was also applied successfully to determine AFB1 in Beer, Rice, and Peanuts, indicating the electrochemical aptasensor has broad application prospects.

Qitong Huang: Conceptualization, Data curation, Writing – original draft, Funding acquisition. Xiaofeng Lin: Data collection, Validation. Dejian Chen: Data collection. Qing-Xiao Tong: Conceptualization, Writing – review & editing, Funding acquisition.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This research was supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (Nos. 82060599, 51973107, and 51673113), the Natural Science Foundation of Jiangxi Province (No. 20202BABL213018), the Science and Technology Project of Jiangxi Health Committee (No. 202131033), the Science and Technology Project of the Education Department of Jiangxi Province (No. GJJ190795), the Research Fund of Gannan Medical University (ZD201901, YQ202003), the Key Project of GDUPS (2019) and the Key Project of DEGP (No. 2018KZDXM032).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.foodchem.2021.131415.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: The following are the Supplementary data to this article:

TEM image of CDs (Fig. S1A), the Fluorescence spectrum of CDs (Fig. S1B). IR spectrum of CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite (Fig. S2). XRD spectrum of CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite (Fig. S3). Magnetization curve of CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4 nanocomposite measured at 300 K (Fig. S4). EIS of GCE, CDs/GCE, CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE and S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE in 1.0 mM [Fe(CN)6]3-/4- and 0.1 M KCl (Fig. S5). EIS of S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE and AFB1-S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE in 1.0 mM [Fe(CN)6]3-/4- and 0.1 M KCl (Fig. S6). DPV response of the interaction time of MB with the S1 (Fig. S7). CVs of S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE in 10 μM MB and 0.1 M PBS (pH 7.0) at the scan rates of 0.010, 0.025, 0.050, 0.075, 0.10, 0.125, 0.15, 0.175, 0.20, 0.225, 0.25, 0.275, 0.30 V s−1 (Fig. S8A), linear relationship between I and v (Fig. S8B). The stability of AFB1 was determined by S1/CDs/α-Fe2O3-Fe3O4/GCE (Fig. S9).
Supplementary data 1

ce:bib-reference.ce:label: Aftab et al., 2019

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Carbon quantum dots co-catalyzed with multiwalled carbon nanotubes and silver nanoparticles modified nanosensor for the electrochemical assay of anti-HIV drug Rilpivirine
Sensors and Actuators B: Chemical

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Aftab, S., Kurbanoglu, S., Ozcelikay, G., Bakirhan, N. K., Shah, A., & Ozkan, S. A. (2019). Carbon quantum dots co-catalyzed with multiwalled carbon nanotubes and silver nanoparticles modified nanosensor for the electrochemical assay of anti-HIV drug Rilpivirine.
